name: Creator Publishing Lifecycle Reference Cluster
description: Explicitly routes a creator decision to one owning judgment asset and at most two distinct advisors across positioning, topic choice, originality, audience evidence, depth, and sustainable publishing cadence.

domains.load_condition: Brand positioning, known for, remembered as, readers, audience, differentiation, niche value, creator identity, reposition, pivot, direction, 品牌定位、被记住、读者、受众心智、差异化、细分价值、创作者身份、转型、方向。
Topic, idea, candidate subject, trend, hot issue, angle, 选题、创意、候选话题、热点、角度。
Draft publication, original insight, distinct value, sources, curation, publish-worthiness, 草稿发布、原创洞察、独特价值、来源、策展、是否值得发布。
Publishing cadence, schedule, frequency, daily, weekly, consistency, capacity, 发布节奏、排期、频率、日更、周更、规律、产能。
Research depth, length, scope, beginner, expert, explanation, evidence burden, 研究深度、篇幅、范围、新手、专家、解释、证据负担。
domains.contribution_hypothesis_template: For {task}, define the audience-specific cognitive job and minimum sufficient depth that the owning primary does not determine.

domains.load_condition: Metrics, comments, retention, completion rate, conversion, feedback, audience behavior, 指标、评论、留存、完播率、转化、反馈、受众行为。
domains.contribution_hypothesis_template: For {task}, test the primary's assumption against objective-matched audience evidence and name alternative explanations before changing direction.

relationships.description: A topic may extend the position but should not silently redefine its audience or value.
Feasible depth and evidence burden affect whether a topic is worth committing to now.
Depth tests whether a distinct claim is sufficiently developed for its audience.
Repeated audience evidence can test whether intended positioning is actually perceived.
Objective-matched response data can reveal whether cadence is degrading quality or expectation.
